description: Start your meal with these delicious bites.

items.name: Sinulog na Isda
items.description: Traditional fish stew with local spices, served with steamed rice.

items.name: Hinava
items.description: Indigenous ceviche made with fresh river fish, lime, and chili.

items.name: Kinilaw
items.description: Raw fish marinated in vinegar, lime, and ginger, a Filipino favorite.

description: Hearty dishes that define Tagum's culinary identity.

items.name: Sinagapona Kagan Food
items.description: Sinagapona is commonly served with rice and enjoyed during family meals and cultural gatherings, reflecting the Kagan community’s connection to their natural resources and traditional cooking practices.

description: Sweet treats to end your meal on a high note.

items.name: Amik, Muslim Food
items.description: Amik is a traditional Muslim sweet delicacy made from rice flour and sugar, formed into a delicate lace-like pattern when fried.

items.name: Daral
items.description: Daral is a sweet rolled crepe-like delicacy made from thin rice flour pancakes filled with sweetened coconut (usually mixed with brown sugar).

items.name: Pitis
items.description: Pitis is a sweet rice-based delicacy usually made from glutinous rice, coconut milk, and sugar. It has a soft and sticky texture and is often wrapped in banana leaves or served in small portions.

description: Quick and flavorful snacks you can find in local markets.

items.name: Isaw
items.description: Grilled chicken intestines, a popular street food skewer.

items.name: Kwek-Kwek
items.description: Deep-fried quail eggs coated in orange batter, served with vinegar dip.

items.name: Fish Ball
items.description: Deep-fried fish balls served with sweet and spicy sauce.
